hero.question: LoL: HANJIN BRION Challengers vs DN SOOPers Challengers - Game 2 Winner
hero.prob_take: At 6.5%, the crowd is pricing HANJIN BRION as nearly eliminated, which means you need extreme confidence in a massive upset to get paid here. DN SOOPers is the heavy favorite, but Korean Challengers matches have a history of closer competition than the odds suggest, and 6.5% is thin enough that even a 15-20% true win rate would be profitable.

daily_take.headline: Korean League Collapse Signals Sharp Bettors Know Something
daily_take.deck: HANJIN BRION Challengers just cratered 32 points in 24 hours, now sitting at 6.5% to win Game 2. The -32 point swing doesn't happen on air. Sharp money left the building, which means either new intel dropped on roster/performance or the book got way too thin and got arb'd. At 6.5% you're getting paid for certainty that doesn't exist in esports. The play: wait for the dust to settle and look for reversal if this was pure cascading liquidation rather than real information.

sidebar.headline: Iran ceasefire odds stuck in neutral despite Middle East escalation. Market either priced in worst case already or believes April 7 is too soon. Either way, 6.2% with $13.5M behind it smells like conviction, not indecision.
sidebar.label: US-Iran Ceasefire: 6.2%

sidebar.headline: Rublev getting heavy backings at 79.5%, now up 6 points. This is a player line where injury history and clay form matter more than current odds suggest. Check the sharps' line versus this one.
sidebar.label: Rublev vs Borges: 79.5%

sidebar.headline: Strait traffic odds just ticked up 4 points to 15.5%. The volume ($2.9M) is real and the direction matters: markets are pricing in de-escalation by end of April, not breakdown. Follow the institutions here.
sidebar.label: Hormuz Normal Traffic: 15.5%
